info.title: Mobazha Unified API
info.description: Merged OpenAPI spec from Hosting, Node, and Search services.

schema.description: Public provider configuration for linking UI.

get.description: Returns the Casdoor user payload embedded in the session JWT. Requires a Casdoor JWT (API tokens are rejected).

schema.description: Casdoor user object from the session JWT (same shape as legacy /accounts/me).

get.summary: List Deal acceptances requiring order recovery

patch.summary: Patch Managed escrow platform fee configuration (admin)

delete.summary: Remove platform certification for a moderator

get.description: Returns the platform's PEM-encoded JWT verification public key. Standalone stores fetch this once during provisioning so they can validate Casdoor session JWTs locally for SaaS proxy-mediated management requests. Anonymous endpoint by design — the value is a public key.

post.summary: Discord Mini App login via access_token JSON body

get.description: Returns the resolved user ID, peer ID, and effective scopes for the current credential. Used by frontends to render identity UI and by integration tests to verify a token is wired correctly.

Returns the catalogue of scope strings recognised by the platform so token-creation UIs can render checkboxes from a single source of truth. Static for the lifetime of the binary; clients may cache aggressively.

post.description: Exchanges OAuth `code` and `state` for a Casdoor session JWT.

get.summary: Casdoor OAuth callback completing Telegram binding (HTML)

post.description: initData arrives on the upstream query string; JSON body carries sessionId.
post.summary: Finalize binding after Telegram deep-link return
Start Telegram Mini App → Casdoor account binding
Probe whether Telegram Mini App user has an account
Telegram Mini App initData JWT issue

post.description: Query string carries Telegram-signed auth fields (same as legacy POST).
post.summary: Telegram Login Widget verification + JWT issue

get.description: Returns active and revoked tokens belonging to the authenticated user. Token secrets are never echoed; only metadata.

post.description: Mints a new programmatic token bound to the authenticated user. Returns the raw token exactly once — store it immediately. Limit: 25 active tokens per user.

delete.description: Marks the specified token revoked. Subsequent authentication attempts with the token return 401. Idempotent — revoking an already-revoked token is a no-op (still returns 204).

parameters.description: Database ID of the token to revoke.
parameters.name: tokenID

schema.description: Database ID of the token to revoke.

post.description: Creates or idempotently returns a PaymentSession. Fiat: canonical paymentCoin fiat:{provider}:{currency}, fiatAmountCents (>0), and optional PayPal return/cancel URLs. Crypto: optional refundAddress/payerAddress/moderator; buyers should declare refundAddress before paying. Deal cross-currency requests must include paymentSelectionQuoteID from the immutable quote endpoint.

post.description: Runs Core-authorized backend settlement for supported crypto routes. Supported actions: confirm, cancel, seller-decline-refund, complete, dispute-release. Client-signed legacy chains use instruction endpoints. Fiat orders return 400. Optional body: payoutAddress.

certificate.description: PEM-encoded Casdoor JWT verification public key.

expires_in_days.description: Token lifetime in days. Omit for non-expiring.

scopes.description: Scopes or role presets (presets like 'admin:read' are expanded server-side).

token.description: Raw API token. Visible exactly once; treat as a secret.

userId.description: Casdoor user ID of the authenticated caller.

message.description: Static greeting confirming huma is reachable.

serverTime.description: Server time at request handling, RFC3339Nano.

is_api_token.description: True if the credential is a programmatic API token rather than a session JWT.

peer_id.description: Resolved tenant peer ID, when available.

scopes.description: Granted scopes. Session JWTs receive the full catalogue; API tokens receive their declared subset.

action.description: Action verb, e.g. read, write, manage.

active.description: Convenience boolean: true iff not revoked and not expired.

expires_at.description: UTC expiry timestamp. Nil for non-expiring tokens.

id.description: Database ID of the token. Stable per token.

last_used_at.description: UTC timestamp of the most recent successful authentication.

prefix.description: Visible token prefix (mbz_<id>); the secret half is never echoed after creation.

revoked_at.description: UTC revocation timestamp. Nil for active tokens.

scopes.description: Granted scopes. Always populated; empty array if the token has none.

items.description: Per-approval batch action outcomes; correlate entries by approvalId.

reason.description: Human-readable detail when skipped or failed.

result.description: Action outcome: processed, skipped, or failed.

status.description: Approval status after the action, when applicable.

limit.description: Requested row page size (0 means no limit).

offset.description: Requested row offset into the filtered result set.

returnedRows.description: Proposal rows returned in this response page.

status.description: Active row status filter, if any.

totalRows.description: Proposal rows after status filter, before limit/offset pagination.

hasPreview.description: True when source image bytes are available for preview.

actionableCount.description: Rows needing merchant action (full run; ignores status filter).

appliedCount.description: Proposal rows with artifact status applied (full run).

applyingCount.description: Rows with approvals currently applying (full run).

approvedCount.description: Rows with approved but not yet applied approvals (full run).

noApprovalCount.description: Proposal rows without a linked approval (full run).

reviewableCount.description: Proposal rows still in review (full run).

fingerprint.description: Optional machine fingerprint for activation check.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp of the server time.

bearerAuth.description: Casdoor session JWT issued by the platform identity service.

bearerOrAPITokenAuth.description: Either a Casdoor session JWT or an API token created via `POST /platform/v1/auth/tokens`. Both flow as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

adminSession.description: Short-lived HttpOnly standalone administrator session. Unsafe requests also require X-CSRF-Token.
adminSession.name: Mobazha_Admin_Session

basicAuth.description: Standalone admin password via HTTP Basic Auth.

bearerJWT.description: Casdoor JWT issued by the SaaS platform (Mini App / proxy).

nodeAuth.description: Node authentication: Basic Auth (standalone admin), Bearer JWT (SaaS proxy), or Bearer mbz_ API token.
